mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 6.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicates a supply-demand mismatch where demand shifts from goods to services, reducing corporate profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During a rapid monetary tightening cycle, an accelerating rise in the federal funds rate signals that the central bank is successfully cooling demand without breaking the labor market, which supports a soft landing and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly positive during tightening episodes when unemployment remains below 5.5%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in housing starts reflects rising business and consumer confidence, which leads corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between HOUST month-over-month changes and 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.15 over any 3-year window, or the signal generates negative returns in more than 55% of positive HOUST months.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 235.0, it signals entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when CPI crosses the 235.0 threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 45% over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment growth accelerates while industrial production stagnates indicates a structural shift toward services, which reduces manufacturing sector profitability and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS growth and INDPRO stagnation fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the cross-product term's t-statistic remains below 1.5 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a supply-constrained environment where industrial production exceeds 101.0, a simultaneous drop in real consumer credit balances indicates deleveraging that will suppress aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX does not decline following the joint signal of high INDPRO and falling RSAFS, or the predictive power of the combined signal disappears when GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annualized.
